content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.5-xhigh

## Summary

Found two correctness issues in proposed-bead reconciliation: final-review duplicate sources are undercounted for thresholding, and NDJSON idempotency is broader than the specified amendment_id marker.

## Amendments

1. src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1168 records one TerminalReviewFinding per accepted canonical final-review amendment, then src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1457 applies the threshold to that count. Duplicate reviewer findings are merged into one canonical amendment with multiple sources in src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:681, so two reviewers proposing the same body/summary do not meet the default threshold of 2 and no proposed-beads.ndjson record is written.
   Classification: fix_current_bead
   Rationale: Threshold gating is supposed to count findings with the same proposed_bead_summary, not post-dedup canonical amendments.
2. src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1207 tracks existing summary_keys and src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1483 skips any later threshold-met group with the same normalized summary. The idempotency requirement only says to skip records whose amendment_id is already present; this broader summary-level skip suppresses distinct later terminal runs or current beads with a new amendment_id but the same summary, so their source_run_id/current_bead_id/count handoff is never appended.
   Classification: fix_current_bead
   Rationale: Over-broad idempotency can drop legitimate proposed-bead records beyond replay deduplication.
